# solution.py
"""Team Collaboration Manager.

A thread-safe, in-memory backend for a small team collaboration product.

The module exposes a single facade -- :class:`TeamCollaborationManager` -- that
coordinates six cooperating services (users, projects, tasks, messages,
performance and reports).  All services share one re-entrant lock and one
global identity counter so that:

* every entity (user, project, task, message, rating) receives a globally
  unique, monotonically increasing integer id starting at ``1``;
* concurrent callers never corrupt shared indexes or hand out duplicate ids;
* getters return deep copies, so mutating a returned object can never leak
  back into the authoritative store.

Time is injected through a ``now`` callable, which makes the behaviour fully
deterministic under test (a frozen clock) while defaulting to
``datetime.now`` in production.

Only the Python standard library is used.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task may occupy."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorization roles a user may hold."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for generated reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kinds of report the system can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails a validation rule."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class User:
    """A registered member of the collaboration platform."""

    __slots__ = ("id", "username", "email", "role", "created_at")

    def __init__(self, id, username, email, role, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.role = role
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "User(id={0!r}, username={1!r}, role={2!r})".format(
            self.id, self.username, self.role
        )


class Project:
    """A unit of work owned by a single user and bounded in time."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "name",
        "description",
        "start_date",
        "end_date",
        "owner_id",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self, id, name, description, start_date, end_date, owner_id, created_at
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.name = name
        self.description = description
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Project(id={0!r}, name={1!r})".format(self.id, self.name)


class Task:
    """A single actionable item belonging to a project."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "project_id",
        "title",
        "description",
        "assignee_id",
        "deadline",
        "status",
        "created_at",
        "started_at",
        "completed_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        project_id,
        title,
        description,
        assignee_id,
        deadline,
        status,
        created_at,
        started_at=None,
        completed_at=None,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.title = title
        self.description = description
        self.assignee_id = assignee_id
        self.deadline = deadline
        self.status = status
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.started_at = started_at
        self.completed_at = completed_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Task(id={0!r}, title={1!r}, status={2!r})".format(
            self.id, self.title, self.status
        )


class Attachment:
    """File metadata associated with a message."""

    __slots__ = ("filename", "size", "uploaded_by", "uploaded_at")

    def __init__(self, filename, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at):
        self.filename = filename
        self.size = size
        self.uploaded_by = uploaded_by
        self.uploaded_at = uploaded_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Attachment(filename={0!r}, size={1!r})".format(
            self.filename, self.size
        )


class Message:
    """A discussion entry attached to either a project or a task."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "author_id",
        "body",
        "project_id",
        "task_id",
        "parent_id",
        "attachments",
        "created_at",
        "edited_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self,
        id,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id,
        task_id,
        parent_id,
        attachments,
        created_at,
        edited_at=None,
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.author_id = author_id
        self.body = body
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.parent_id = parent_id
        self.attachments = attachments
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.edited_at = edited_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Message(id={0!r}, author_id={1!r})".format(
            self.id, self.author_id
        )


class Rating:
    """A peer rating of one user's performance by another."""

    __slots__ = (
        "id",
        "rater_id",
        "ratee_id",
        "score",
        "feedback",
        "task_id",
        "created_at",
    )

    def __init__(
        self, id, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback, task_id, created_at
    ):
        self.id = id
        self.rater_id = rater_id
        self.ratee_id = ratee_id
        self.score = score
        self.feedback = feedback
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Rating(id={0!r}, score={1!r})".format(self.id, self.score)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _clone(obj):
    """Return a deep copy so callers cannot mutate stored state."""

    return copy.deepcopy(obj)


def _clone_all(items):
    """Return a fresh list of deep copies."""

    return [copy.deepcopy(item) for item in items]


def _is_blank(value):
    """True when ``value`` is not a string or is empty/whitespace only."""

    return not isinstance(value, str) or value.strip() == ""


def _valid_email(email):
    """A deliberately small but sufficient email sanity check."""

    return isinstance(email, str) and email.strip() != "" and "@" in email


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Store:
    """Authoritative in-memory state shared by every service.

    The store owns the single re-entrant lock and the single id counter.  All
    services hold a reference to the same store instance, which is what allows
    cross-entity operations (for example deleting a project and cascading to
    its tasks and messages) to execute atomically under one lock.
    """

    def __init__(self, now):
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._now = now
        self._id_counter = 0
        self.users = {}
        self.projects = {}
        self.tasks = {}
        self.messages = {}
        self.ratings = {}

    # -- id + time ----------------------------------------------------------
    def next_id(self):
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    def now(self):
        return self._now()

    # -- existence guards (assume the caller already holds the lock) --------
    def require_user(self, user_id):
        user = self.users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def require_project(self, project_id):
        project = self.projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def require_task(self, task_id):
        task = self.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def require_message(self, message_id):
        message = self.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    # -- shared query helpers ----------------------------------------------
    def tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id):
        return [t for t in self.tasks.values() if t.assignee_id == user_id]

    def tasks_for_project(self, project_id):
        return [t for t in self.tasks.values() if t.project_id == project_id]

    def ratings_for_ratee(self, user_id):
        return [r for r in self.ratings.values() if r.ratee_id == user_id]


class _Service:
    """Base class giving every service the shared store and lock."""

    def __init__(self, store):
        self._store = store
        self._lock = store._lock


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _UserService(_Service):
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        with self._lock:
            if _is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("Username must be a non-empty string")
            if not _valid_email(email):
                raise ValidationError("Email must contain '@'")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("Role must be a UserRole")
            store = self._store
            if any(u.username == username for u in store.users.values()):
                raise DuplicateError("Username already taken")
            if any(u.email == email for u in store.users.values()):
                raise DuplicateError("Email already registered")
            user = User(store.next_id(), username, email, role, store.now())
            store.users[user.id] = user
            return _clone(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._store.require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self):
        with self._lock:
            users = sorted(self._store.users.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return _clone_all(users)

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            user = store.require_user(user_id)
            if username is not None:
                if _is_blank(username):
                    raise ValidationError("Username must be a non-empty string")
                if any(
                    u.id != user_id and u.username == username
                    for u in store.users.values()
                ):
                    raise DuplicateError("Username already taken")
            if email is not None:
                if not _valid_email(email):
                    raise ValidationError("Email must contain '@'")
                if any(
                    u.id != user_id and u.email == email
                    for u in store.users.values()
                ):
                    raise DuplicateError("Email already registered")
            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("Role must be a UserRole")
            if username is not None:
                user.username = username
            if email is not None:
                user.email = email
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role
            return _clone(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            if any(p.owner_id == user_id for p in store.projects.values()):
                raise ConflictError("User still owns one or more projects")
            if any(
                t.assignee_id == user_id and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                for t in store.tasks.values()
            ):
                raise ConflictError("User has open assigned tasks")
            del store.users[user_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _ProjectService(_Service):
    def create_project(
        self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""
    ):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(owner_id)
            if _is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("Project name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")
            project = Project(
                store.next_id(),
                name,
                description if description is not None else "",
                start_date,
                end_date,
                owner_id,
                store.now(),
            )
            store.projects[project.id] = project
            return _clone(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._store.require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self):
        with self._lock:
            projects = sorted(
                self._store.projects.values(), key=lambda p: p.id
            )
            return _clone_all(projects)

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=None,
        description=None,
        start_date=None,
        end_date=None,
    ):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._store.require_project(project_id)
            new_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            new_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if name is not None and _is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("Project name must be a non-empty string")
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")
            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description
            if start_date is not None:
                project.start_date = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                project.end_date = end_date
            return _clone(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = [
                tid
                for tid, t in store.tasks.items()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            task_id_set = set(task_ids)
            for tid in task_ids:
                del store.tasks[tid]
            message_ids = [
                mid
                for mid, m in store.messages.items()
                if m.project_id == project_id or m.task_id in task_id_set
            ]
            for mid in message_ids:
                del store.messages[mid]
            del store.projects[project_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _TaskService(_Service):
    def create_task(
        self, project_id, title, description="", assignee_id=None, deadline=None
    ):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            project = store.require_project(project_id)
            if _is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("Task title must be a non-empty string")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                store.require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("Deadline cannot precede project start")
            task = Task(
                store.next_id(),
                project_id,
                title,
                description if description is not None else "",
                assignee_id,
                deadline,
                TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                store.now(),
            )
            store.tasks[task.id] = task
            return _clone(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            return _clone(self._store.require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_project(project_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                store.tasks_for_project(project_id), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            return _clone_all(tasks)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(assignee_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                store.tasks_for_assignee(assignee_id), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            return _clone_all(tasks)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            task = store.require_task(task_id)
            store.require_user(assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = assignee_id
            return _clone(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            task = store.require_task(task_id)
            store.require_user(new_assignee_id)
            task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
            return _clone(task)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._store.require_task(task_id)
            task.assignee_id = None
            return _clone(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            task = store.require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = store.projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "Deadline cannot precede project start"
                    )
            task.deadline = deadline
            return _clone(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            store = self._store
            task = store.require_task(task_id)
            now = store.now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            task.status = status
            return _clone(task)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _MessageService(_Service):
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        attachments=None,
        parent_id=None,
    ):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            store.require_user(author_id)
            if project_id is not None:
                store.require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                store.require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                store.require_message(parent_id)
            now = store.now()
            attachment_objs = []
            if attachments:
                for filename, data in attachments:
                    attachment_objs.append(
                        Attachment(filename, len(data), author_id, now)
                    )
            body_text = body if body is not None else ""
            if body_text.strip() == "" and not attachment_objs:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "Message requires a body or at least one attachment"
                )
            message = Message(
                store.next_id(),
                author_id,
                body_text,
                project_id,
                task_id,
                parent_id,
                attachment_objs,
                now,
            )
            store.messages[message.id] = message
            return _clone(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            message = store.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("Only the author may edit a message")
            if new_body is None or new_body.strip() == "":
                raise ValidationError("Message body must be non-empty")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = store.now()
            return _clone(message)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, user_id, filename, data):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            message = store.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may attach to a message"
                )
            if _is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("Filename must be a non-empty string")
            message.attachments.append(
                Attachment(filename, len(data), user_id, store.now())
            )
            return _clone(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_project(project_id)
            messages = sorted(
                (
                    m
                    for m in store.messages.values()
                    if m.project_id == project_id
                ),
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return _clone_all(messages)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_task(task_id)
            messages = sorted(
                (m for m in store.messages.values() if m.task_id == task_id),
                key=lambda m: m.id,
            )
            return _clone_all(messages)

    def delete_message(self, message_id, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            message = store.require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != user_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "Only the author may delete a message"
                )
            del store.messages[message_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _PerformanceService(_Service):
    @staticmethod
    def _completion_rate(tasks):
        if not tasks:
            return 0.0
        completed = sum(
            1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
        )
        return completed / len(tasks)

    @staticmethod
    def _average_completion_time(tasks):
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.completed_at is not None
            and t.started_at is not None
        ]
        if not durations:
            return None
        return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            return self._completion_rate(store.tasks_for_assignee(user_id))

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            return self._average_completion_time(
                store.tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            )

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=None, task_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(rater_id)
            store.require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("Users cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("Score must be an integer 1-5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("Score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                store.require_task(task_id)
            rating = Rating(
                store.next_id(),
                rater_id,
                ratee_id,
                score,
                feedback,
                task_id,
                store.now(),
            )
            store.ratings[rating.id] = rating
            return _clone(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            ratings = sorted(
                store.ratings_for_ratee(user_id), key=lambda r: r.id
            )
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": _clone_all(ratings),
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = store.tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            ratings = store.ratings_for_ratee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Report service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _ReportService(_Service):
    def __init__(self, store, performance):
        super().__init__(store)
        self._performance = performance

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_project(project_id)
            tasks = store.tasks_for_project(project_id)
            now = store.now()
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = status_counts[TaskStatus.COMPLETED.value]
            completion_rate = completed / total if total else 0.0
            overdue = sum(
                1
                for t in tasks
                if t.deadline is not None
                and t.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and now > t.deadline
            )
            per_assignee = {}
            for task in tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                    task.assignee_id,
                    {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "in_progress": 0,
                     "not_started": 0},
                )
                bucket["total"] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    bucket["completed"] += 1
                elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                    bucket["in_progress"] += 1
                else:
                    bucket["not_started"] += 1
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": completion_rate,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            if project_id is not None:
                store.require_project(project_id)
                scoped_tasks = store.tasks_for_project(project_id)
            else:
                scoped_tasks = list(store.tasks.values())
            assignee_ids = sorted(
                {t.assignee_id for t in scoped_tasks if t.assignee_id is not None}
            )
            rows = []
            for user_id in assignee_ids:
                user = store.users.get(user_id)
                user_tasks = [
                    t for t in scoped_tasks if t.assignee_id == user_id
                ]
                ratings = store.ratings_for_ratee(user_id)
                rating_average = (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings)
                    if ratings
                    else 0.0
                )
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": user_id,
                        "username": user.username if user else "",
                        "total_tasks": len(user_tasks),
                        "completed_tasks": sum(
                            1
                            for t in user_tasks
                            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                        ),
                        "completion_rate": _PerformanceService._completion_rate(
                            user_tasks
                        ),
                        "average_completion_time": (
                            _PerformanceService._average_completion_time(
                                user_tasks
                            )
                        ),
                        "rating_count": len(ratings),
                        "rating_average": rating_average,
                    }
                )
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            store = self._store
            store.require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(
                store.tasks_for_assignee(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id
            )
            ratings = store.ratings_for_ratee(user_id)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings)
                if ratings
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": len(tasks),
                "completed_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ),
                "completion_rate": _PerformanceService._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": (
                    _PerformanceService._average_completion_time(tasks)
                ),
                "rating_count": len(ratings),
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": _clone_all(tasks),
            }

    # -- export -------------------------------------------------------------
    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
            raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
        if not isinstance(report, dict):
            raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
        if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
            return self._to_csv(report)
        return self._to_pdf(report)

    @staticmethod
    def _stringify(value):
        if value is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(value, (dict, list)):
            return str(value)
        return str(value)

    def _to_csv(self, report):
        output = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(output)
        report_type = report.get("report_type")
        if report_type == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            rows = report.get("rows", [])
            headers = (
                list(rows[0].keys())
                if rows
                else [
                    "user_id",
                    "username",
                    "total_tasks",
                    "completed_tasks",
                    "completion_rate",
                    "average_completion_time",
                    "rating_count",
                    "rating_average",
                ]
            )
            writer.writerow(headers)
            for row in rows:
                writer.writerow([self._stringify(row.get(h)) for h in headers])
        else:
            writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, self._stringify(value)])
        return output.getvalue()

    def _to_pdf(self, report):
        lines = ["Report: {0}".format(report.get("report_type", "report"))]
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, self._stringify(value)))
        return _render_pdf(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal PDF renderer (standard library only)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _render_pdf(lines):
    """Render ``lines`` of text into a minimal but valid single-page PDF."""

    text_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "50 750 Td", "14 TL"]
    first = True
    for line in lines:
        safe = (
            str(line)
            .replace("\\", "\\\\")
            .replace("(", "\\(")
            .replace(")", "\\)")
        )
        if first:
            text_ops.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
            first = False
        else:
            text_ops.append("T* ({0}) Tj".format(safe))
    text_ops.append("ET")
    content = "\n".join(text_ops).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        (
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>"
        ),
        b"<< /Length "
        + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
        + b" >>\nstream\n"
        + content
        + b"\nendstream",
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
    ]

    pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
    offsets = []
    for index, obj in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(pdf))
        pdf += str(index).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n" + obj + b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_pos = len(pdf)
    size = len(objects) + 1
    pdf += b"xref\n0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
    pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
    pdf += b"trailer\n<< /Size " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
    pdf += b"startxref\n" + str(xref_pos).encode("ascii") + b"\n%%EOF"
    return bytes(pdf)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Facade coordinating all collaboration services under one lock.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        Optional zero-argument callable returning the current
        :class:`datetime.datetime`.  Defaults to :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
        Injecting a deterministic clock makes time-dependent behaviour fully
        testable.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._store = _Store(now if now is not None else datetime.now)
        self._lock = self._store._lock
        self.users = _UserService(self._store)
        self.projects = _ProjectService(self._store)
        self.tasks = _TaskService(self._store)
        self.messages = _MessageService(self._store)
        self.performance = _PerformanceService(self._store)
        self.reports = _ReportService(self._store, self.performance)

    # -- user management ----------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        return self.users.register_user(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.get_user(user_id)

    def list_users(self):
        return self.users.list_users()

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        return self.users.update_user(
            user_id, username=username, email=email, role=role
        )

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.delete_user(user_id)

    # -- project management -------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(
        self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description=""
    ):
        return self.projects.create_project(
            name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description
        )

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.get_project(project_id)

    def list_projects(self):
        return self.projects.list_projects()

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id,
        name=None,
        description=None,
        start_date=None,
        end_date=None,
    ):
        return self.projects.update_project(
            project_id,
            name=name,
            description=description,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.delete_project(project_id)

    # -- task management ----------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(
        self, project_id, title, description="", assignee_id=None, deadline=None
    ):
        return self.tasks.create_task(
            project_id,
            title,
            description=description,
            assignee_id=assignee_id,
            deadline=deadline,
        )

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.get_task(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_assignee(assignee_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, new_assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.reassign_task(task_id, new_assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.unassign_task(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- messaging ----------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id,
        body,
        project_id=None,
        task_id=None,
        attachments=None,
        parent_id=None,
    ):
        return self.messages.post_message(
            author_id,
            body,
            project_id=project_id,
            task_id=task_id,
            attachments=attachments,
            parent_id=parent_id,
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        return self.messages.edit_message(message_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, user_id, filename, data):
        return self.messages.add_attachment(message_id, user_id, filename, data)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id, user_id):
        return self.messages.delete_message(message_id, user_id)

    # -- performance --------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=None, task_id=None):
        return self.performance.submit_rating(
            rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=feedback, task_id=task_id
        )

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- reports ------------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        return self.reports.project_progress_report(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        return self.reports.team_performance_report(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        return self.reports.individual_performance_report(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        return self.reports.export_report(report, report_format)
